purpose: A full summary of this working session: two public GitHub repos built to analyze India's trade data and FDI/PLI policy, the chronological arc of every bulletin produced, and the cross-cutting insights that emerged across the analyses -- including several that only became visible by comparing bulletins against each other, not from any single one alone.

repo_architecture.origin: Both repos were originally one (mospi-dataset-analysis); split so the descriptive 'what does the data show' analysis and the prescriptive 'what should be done about it' recommendations could be published, versioned, and read independently. The original repo was kept, with a banner pointing to the split.

chronological_deliverables.title: PLI report card -- disbursal grades, IEM record, PIB-credited companies, Exim cross-check
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: Graded the government's own side of the PLI bargain: incentive disbursal stands at Rs 28,748cr across all 14 sectors as of 31 Dec 2025 -- roughly 15% of the Rs 1.91-1.97 lakh cr outlay (PIB states the outlay inconsistently across its own releases) -- verified against Parliament-answer PRIDs, not press. Grades: Pharma A (36% of outlay disbursed, sales Rs 3.19 lakh cr), Electronics A- (every outcome target exceeded, Rs 15,554cr disbursed, smartphones India's #1 export item CY2025), down to Specialty Steel D (Rs 48cr = 0.8% disbursed, and PIB discloses 14 of 58 Round-1 projects WITHDREW -- attrition absent from the Ministry's own site) and ACC Battery F (zero disbursed; Ola's 1 GWh still 'pilot production' since Mar 2024 in PIB's own words). IEM record graded B+ on the 82%->109.9% implementation-ratio surge. PIB-credited producing companies listed per scheme with PRIDs, attribution kept honest -- Lyfius/Kinvan/Meril identities are corporate-filing/press, not PIB, and Textiles/Steel/Food Processing get zero company names in PIB at all: the underperforming schemes are also the anonymous ones. Independent Exim Bank cross-check: FY26 trade deficit $333.2bn matches the repo's computed $334.3bn within 0.3%; the deficit widened 17.5% in a single year; bank credit to PLI sectors accelerating (Electronics +23.9%, Vehicles +26.4% YoY). Also added as slide 21 of the FDI pitch deck (now 22 slides, PPTX/PDF rebuilt): the five grade buckets as color-coded columns, framed for investors as 'the grade is the probability the scheme's money actually arrives.'

chronological_deliverables.title: Report-card template + master report (the source-of-truth document)
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: Two closing infrastructure pieces. (1) A report-card template added to templates/ (grade badges A-F with evidence rows, named-entities chips, pattern callout, corrections block -- encoding the repo's conventions: grades are judgment, evidence is the checkable part, an F never rests on paraphrase alone), with pre-built PPTX/PDF. (2) The MASTER REPORT (data/master_report_2026-07-19.json -> India_Trade_Master_Report_2026-07-19.docx/.pdf in reports/published/, 14 pages, 12 data tables): every finding, figure, grade and correction from the program in one document across nine parts -- trade position, sector verdicts with full FDI series, the 13-scheme graded PLI assessment, IEM record, companies ledger with PRIDs, capital/financing analysis, data-infrastructure assessment, action items, corrections ledger. Designated the source of truth from which the pitch deck and dashboards update. A research pass filling the four disbursal gaps (Bulk Drugs, Medical Devices, Telecom, Solar PV) via Lok Sabha/Rajya Sabha answers was in flight as of this phase.

chronological_deliverables.title: State-wise investment implementation (domestic+foreign, IEM Part B) + sector leaders
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: Built the state table the FDI data could never provide: implemented industrial investment 2021-2025 combining domestic and foreign capital, on IEM Part B (Rs 19.52 lakh cr across 4,859 projects). Gujarat alone is 33.8% (Rs 4.55 lakh cr in 2024-25 alone, 178% intent-to-implementation conversion); top 3 states = 63.3%. The IEM-vs-FDI contrast quantifies the financing thesis per state: Odisha #2 in implementation with 0.14% of FDI; Karnataka the inversion (22% of FDI, 2.2% of implementation); Assam's 2023 Tata-era Rs 18,778cr registers in IEM against $2.37M FDI. Sector leaders: Metallurgical Industries #1 (24.1%, Rs 2.09 lakh cr in 2025 alone) and Chemicals #2 (14.7%) WITH NO PLI SCHEME -- private chemical capex landing at scale regardless. Master report gained part 2b.

chronological_deliverables.title: Disbursal gaps filled via Parliament answers; three grades revised in the open
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: Lok Sabha/Rajya Sabha written replies filled all four disbursal gaps: Medical Devices Rs 157.15cr (4.6%, LS 13 Mar 2026), Solar PV zero BY DESIGN (payable 1yr post-commissioning, while ~30 GW module capacity is commissioned -- the opposite of ACC's execution-failure zero), Telecom ~Rs 1,175cr through FY25 and accelerating, Bulk Drugs ~Rs 55cr (0.8%, secondary-sourced and flagged). Three grades revised with the revisions shown: Bulk Drugs A- -> B, Medical Devices B+ -> B, Solar C+ -> B-. Also captured the FY2024-25 per-scheme flow split (Rs 10,114cr) and a working route into sansad.in answer PDFs for future collectors. Deck slide and master report updated.

chronological_deliverables.title: Project workflow & data-sourcing schema
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: Documented the program's own architecture as a bulletin: the seven-gate investment-lifecycle measurement schema (announcement -> IEM Part A -> land/EC via PARIVESH -> IEM Part B -> PLI disbursal -> FDI equity -> trade outcome, with the official source and blind spot at each gate), the 14-source data inventory with access methods, the seven-step working method, and the encountered failure modes. The map for anyone extending or refreshing the repo. PARIVESH EC-pipeline research (gate 3) in flight.

chronological_deliverables.title: PARIVESH EC pipeline -- gate 3 goes live
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: Discovered that PARIVESH 2.0 exposes a fully open JSON API returning the complete national Environmental Clearance register in one call (113,804 proposals, SEIAA state-level clearances included -- the feared Category-B gap is dead). Built the gate-3 bulletin: Gujarat holds ~31% of industrial EC grants AND the deepest ToR pipeline (corroborating and predicting its 33.8% IEM implementation share); Maharashtra is the most forward-loaded state (ToR > granted); grant flow accelerating in step with the IEM ratio's break to 109.9%. Sector split corroborates the implementation leaders (synthetic organic chemicals 1,192 granted with Gujarat at 56%; metallurgy 641, multi-state). Flagship reads with proposal numbers: Dholera fab and Assam ATMP at ToR stage, Micron/Kaynes/Reliance-electrolyser cleared, all three Bulk Drug Parks and three PM MITRA parks cleared -- and two negative findings that matter as much: POSCO-JSW's $7.73bn JV has no EC application at all, and RRPCL is entirely absent, clearance-register proof of the stalled-at-MoU verdict. Lifecycle schema gate 3 updated to live; the API re-pull added to the quarterly checklist; master report gained part 2c. Also added as slide 22 of the FDI pitch deck (now 23 slides, PPTX/PDF rebuilt): the state pipeline bars, flagship clearance statuses, and the absences-are-due-diligence callout, framed for investors as 'any Indian project's clearance reality is one API call away.' Correlation then quantified: EC granted vs Part B implemented value (2024-25) across 16 states = Pearson 0.961 / Spearman 0.794 / log-log 0.864 -- clearance stock explains ~92% of variance in implementation levels (not growth rates), with residuals naming the over-performers (AP, MP, Odisha) and under-performers (Jharkhand, Assam, Karnataka -0.35 -- its pipeline-building read quantified).

chronological_deliverables.title: The complete compendium -- one PDF for the whole program
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: Compiled everything into a single coherent 140-page PDF (India_Trade_Compendium_2026-07-19.pdf, tracked in reports/published/ and copied to Downloads): generated cover + page-numbered TOC + PDF bookmarks; the master report as the narrative spine; the workflow & data-sourcing schema; all 16 bulletins printed pixel-faithfully from their live HTML in lifecycle order; and the 23-slide FDI pitch deck as the closing appendix. Built with the repo's own print pipeline, TOC page numbers computed from actual per-section counts and verified.

chronological_deliverables.title: The narrative abstract -- why import substitution, told in eight acts
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: Built the program's opening frame as an 8-act narrative (per the plan-then-write narrative-function model, arXiv:2406.00554): the $333.2bn deficit and rupee slide as the stakes; PLI as the plan; PIB-verified ethanol blending as the import-substitution precedent (1.53%->20% blending 'five years ahead of schedule' per Factsheet 150699, Rs 1.97 lakh cr forex saved per PRID 2283118, with MoPNG's own admitted downsides kept); and the repo's findings as conflict (15% disbursed, greens reversed), twist (defective official data), turning point (109.9% implementation ratio, EC-IEM r=0.96, Pharma clean win), resolution (six actions) and continuation. Wired as the first section of the compendium, README opener, and index card.

chronological_deliverables.title: Quarterly refresh blueprint -- TOGAF layers x modern data architecture
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: Upgraded the quarterly workflow from a checklist to an operating architecture, merging the maintainer's TOGAF blueprint (business/data/application/technology/operations layering, backlog, success metrics) with the Modern Data Architecture playbook: 14-source ingestion layer, immutable dated data/*.json snapshots (corrections ledger as audit log), per-gate CHANGE-DATA-CAPTURE deltas instead of full re-scans (with the dedup mandate: PIB cumulative figures supersede, never sum), a Lambda speed layer for mid-quarter events, the seven-step regeneration pipeline, refresh months Jul/Oct/Jan/Apr, week-by-week runbook, five encountered failure modes with standing responses, and a prioritized automation backlog. Cadence mapped onto SAFe for Government (each refresh = a Program Increment; verification gates = quality-in-flow; corrections ledger = the governance artifact). QUARTERLY_CHECKLIST.md declared the operator view of the blueprint.

chronological_deliverables.title: PIB date-wise x ministry SQL index (ships the blueprint's top backlog item)
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: scripts/pib_index.py + data/pib_index.sqlite: 9,657 PIB press releases (2026 YTD) indexed date-wise by ministry with PRID/title/url, the PIB factsheet listing (22), and a cited_in_repo flag -- every PRID and factsheet cited in the repo's data JSONs (32) resolves to an indexed row, all 22 pre-2026 citations back-filled with date/ministry/title from detail pages. Key access discovery: POST to www.pib.gov.in, never the bare domain -- the 301 redirect turns POSTs into GETs and ASP.NET filters silently reset to today (looks like token rejection, is not); one day-page request covers all ministries via <h3> grouping. Quarterly delta is now one command: --update --factsheets --seed-cited.

chronological_deliverables.title: Beyond PLI -- the 2026 non-PLI incentive wave (first bulletin FROM the PIB index)
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: Swept the full 2026 Cabinet register from the new PIB index: ~Rs 1.9 lakh crore of commitments that deliberately avoid production-linked design (Urban Challenge Fund Rs 1L cr, gasification VGF Rs 37,500cr, BHAVYA parks Rs 33,660cr, BMI maritime insurance pool Rs 12,980cr, ECLGS 5.0, NIPU-2026) -- de-risking inputs where PLI pays for output. Three of the four repo-flagged import gaps (fuels/fertilisers/cotton + pulses) received 2026 instruments; plastics and inorganic chemicals remain the only large uncovered heads.

chronological_deliverables.title: Five-year incentive evolution 2021-2026 -- 93 launches via 5 parallel backfills + 5 classification agents
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: PIB index extended to 83,734 releases (2020-2026) by five parallel year-workers; five agents classified each year's Cabinet register into 93 incentive launches. The 2026 pivot is a TREND: new production-linked schemes 11 (2021) -> 1 -> 1 -> 0 (2024) -> 2 -> 0; production-linked was only 11.2% of new-commitment money even at the 2021 peak (infra missions carried 6x); the 'linked' payout base migrated output -> design -> employment (ELI 2025 Rs 99,446cr, larger than any single PLI); coal gasification escalated 4.4x on the repo-flagged gap; a critical-minerals chain assembled in 2025; maritime became a Rs 69,725cr package sector with no PLI.

chronological_deliverables.title: Scheme registry -- launch dates, windows, applicants, and how the closed ones closed
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: 16-entry PLI-family registry (Cabinet launch PRIDs, incentive windows, officially-reported applicant counts with 'not published' recorded as data) + closures timeline: LSEM first to complete a tenure (31 Mar 2026 -> MPMS); Drones first to expire with no successor (31 Mar 2024); ACC stalled at zero with a vanished 20 GWh awardee; Steel 14-of-58 withdrawal; White Goods 85->80 unexplained; ECLGS lapsed then revived; next watch: Food Processing sunset FY27. 836 approved applications programme-wide but only ~half the schemes ever had per-scheme counts published.

chronological_deliverables.title: Ministry incentive catalog -- 16 ministries + NSWS, site-verified by six agents
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: ~100 company-facing policies verified on ministry sites/CMS APIs/portals. Access-architecture discovery: MeitY/DoT/DPIIT are JS shells whose real content lives in public wp-json CMS APIs; fert.nic.in + texmin.nic.in DNS-dead, mines.gov.in unreachable, msme.gov.in scheme list unrenderable -- 4 of 16 ministries cannot answer 'what schemes do you run' via their web estate. Six scheme pages provably stale. Open-now windows cluster in capex/cluster/credit lanes (UNNATI to 30-Sep-2026, gasification R1, DoP API-CF/SMDI, ECMS extended). NSWS verified (32 ministries/34 states; 6 scheme gateways) -- contributed IFLDP's Rs 1,700cr sub-scheme rates and the only formal closure notice of its application window found anywhere. DISPLAYED CORRECTION to the registry: PM E-DRIVE extended segment-wise (e-2W 31-Jul-2026, e-3W R/C 31-Mar-2028), not ended -- the live portal beat the announced window. Session-limit deaths of all six agents were recovered by relaunch after reset + transcript-resume after a process restart.

chronological_deliverables.title: Investor workflow -- from sector to sanction via NSWS
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: Investor-facing capstone: eight steps (sector->instrument mapping, portal-not-page window checks, PN3 Beneficial-Owner structuring, NSWS KYA + single window, per-scheme portals with PMA contacts, seven statutory gates with PARIVESH EC as the 0.96 forward indicator, calibrated claims from the report card, quarterly monitoring), a ministry-connects table (IFCI/C-DAC/SECI/IREDA/NICDC/state SPVs/NSWS helpline), a candor layer (three-numbers discipline, ~15% five-year PLI disbursal, attrition precedents, 40-50% state top-ups), and the dated trigger calendar (UNNATI 30-Sep-2026, E20 decision 31-Oct-2026, ALMM List-II 31-Dec-2026, PLISFPI sunset FY27).

chronological_deliverables.title: Simplified quarterly PDF on the NITI Trade Watch template + linked-movements roster
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: Replaced the fragile 19-section pixel-faithful compendium pipeline (two passes, Chrome pre-execution, a 6-hour WeasyPrint layout hang, ~3h runtime) with build_quarterly_report.py: one generated HTML rendered in a single WeasyPrint pass (~1 second, 12 pages) structured on NITI Aayog's Trade Watch Quarterly template -- Highlights, the recurring linked-movements roster (each row pairs a policy movement with the trade movement it answers and the forex link), merchandise performance, partners, FY26 verdicts, special theme, matured-schemes section, policy developments with displayed corrections, lifecycle monitor, statistical annex. Quarterly refresh = edit sourced figures and re-run.

chronological_deliverables.title: The matured cohort -- 18 schemes traced launch-to-outcome (2008-2020 -> 2026)
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: PIB register extended to 122,141 releases (2017-2026) by four parallel backfills (modern portal's day-wise boundary is 2017 -- recorded honestly; pre-2017 launches traced through later releases, the ethanol method). Three tracer agents followed 18 major schemes with PRID-cited trajectories: scoreboard 8 closed-success / 8 partial / 2 laggards. Core finding: every closed-success ran on a demand-side guarantee or procurement design (ethanol offtake Rs 1.97L cr saved; UJALA bulk procurement Rs 310->38 LED crash; coal revenue-share auctions; UDAN VGF auctions 74->165 airports; MUDRA guarantees Rs 40L cr; M-SIPS+PMP->LSEM->MPMS: mobile production 28x, exports 127x, imports 75% of demand -> 0.02%). Laggards: SATAT (50 of 5,000 plants -- pivoted to mandate) and BharatNet (three redesigns). Design beats sector; misses are never announced; employment-linked payouts have decade-old roots (PMRPY/ABRY -> ELI); honest instrument-to-loop timescale ~8-10 years. Two further agents classified the 2017-2020 Cabinet registers (61 launches), completing the 2017-2026 launch dataset at 154 launches.

chronological_deliverables.title: Retrospective quarterly series FY2014-15 -> FY2026-27 (13 documents)
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: build_quarterly_series.py generates 13 one-page Trade-Watch PDFs, one per fiscal year: quarterly policy launches (PRID-cited from the classified register + traced 2014-16 cohort), verified anchors per year (deficit series -191 -> -333; blending 1.53% -> 20%; mobile factories 2 -> 300+; solar 2.82 -> 100.33 GW; MUDRA Rs 1.37L cr -> Rs 40L cr; FDI $62bn -> $81bn plateau), and a policy<->trade<->forex linkage line per year -- the linked-movements roster format run backward through the decade. Years the register cannot reach carry fewer anchors, stated plainly.

chronological_deliverables.title: The decade report card -- 2014-2026 graded, open windows, ministries to approach
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: The program's capstone one-pager in three parts: (1) twelve years of policy graded on delivered outcomes -- the A-row (ethanol, M-SIPS->LSEM mobiles, UJALA, MUDRA, Startup India, UDAN, FAME, coal auctions, PLI Pharma) is uniformly demand-side guarantee/procurement designs, the D-F row (SATAT, BharatNet, Auto/Steel/Textiles disbursal, ACC's zero) is voluntary uptake and unpaid production promises, and the 2023-26 wave is marked IN-FLIGHT on the cohort's ~8-10-year loop timescale; (2) portal-verified open investment windows with deadlines (PM E-DRIVE e-2W 31-Jul-2026, UNNATI 30-Sep-2026, ALMM List-II 31-Dec-2026, gasification R1 live, DoP API-CF/SMDI calls, ECMS extended, MPMS Rs 62,500cr window imminent); (3) ministries ranked for investors -- Tier 1 DoP/MeitY/DPIIT-NSWS/FAHD/Coal (windows + delivery + working portals), Tier 2 MoFPI/MNRE/MoPSW/Commerce/MSME-via-portals, eyes-open MHI/Steel/Textiles and the dead-website departments (Fertilizers, Mines) reachable via Cabinet frameworks and NSWS.

chronological_deliverables.title: New repo: focus-sector-investor-map -- companies x government initiatives
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: Bridged the policy program and the market-data stack in a new public repo: the 38,495-company multi-market universe joined to Damodaran's 48,156-company industry classification and mapped into the program's 12 focus sectors -> 894 liquid, market-validated companies -> 175-name shortlist verified for profitability PER NAME via yfinance (147 PROFITABLE / 17 not / 11 unverified excluded, not assumed; 102 with the stated global-expansion signal) -> plus the foreign-investor x initiative pairing table: 12 PRID-verified pairs (Foxconn/Samsung->LSEM->MPMS, Micron/PSMC/Renesas->ISM, First Solar->PLI-T2/ALMM, AM/NS->Specialty Steel, Suzuki->PLI-Auto), 7 prospective lanes (Semicon 2.0 equipment pillar->AMAT/LRCX, shipbuilding package, NIPU-2026, ACC re-tender eyes-open), and retained negative findings (POSCO-JSW's missing EC application, Hyundai's lapsed ACC award, White Goods attrition). Fully reproducible via two scripts; every proxy and join limitation stated in the README.

chronological_deliverables.title: Investor map bulletin (coverage-first, global) + master ministry list
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: Rebuilt the investor map coverage-first per direction: the full Damodaran classification universe mapped to the 12 incentivized sectors WITHOUT a profitability gate -- 19,795 companies across 107 countries (profitability stays a per-name attribute, 147 verified). Published as a policy-repo bulletin: per-sector cards showing global pool -> India's pool -> the incentive lane, with the courting pools matching the FDI-sourcing strategies one-for-one (TW 789 + KR 487 + CN 837 electronics for ECMS/Semicon 2.0; CA 833 + AU 515 metals for the critical-minerals chain; US pharma 724/medtech 204; VN/ID as shipbuilding peers) and chemicals & plastics as the standout asymmetry (2,453 global / 481 India / no scheme). Plus the MASTER MINISTRY LIST: all 86 ministries/departments the government publishes under, derived from the program's own 122k-release register with activity counts -- 16 flagged CATALOGED from the incentive sweep, extending to the remaining 70 (Finance, Defence, S&T, Agriculture, Power, MoHUA, Road Transport...) is the stated next step.

chronological_deliverables.title: digital-twin-for-ipa: full 86-entity sweep, interlinkage graph, PRS budget layer -- new public repo
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: The remaining 70 ministries were swept by 8 parallel agents and the whole program re-packaged as a machine-readable digital twin of India's Investment Promotion Apparatus: 11 layers, with all 86 PIB-register entities now carrying verdicts (HAS-INCENTIVES / THIN / N/A / SUPERSEDED). Strongest instruments surfaced that were in no prior brief: PM-SETU's Rs 10,000cr industry-partner tranche inside the Rs 60,000cr ITI upgradation (Apr-2026), BESS VGF via PSDF (Jul-2025), the OPEN RDI Fund CFP-2026 on tdb.gov.in, five tradeable EPR certificate markets (plastic/e-waste/battery/tyres/used-oil -- recyclers sell certificates to obligated producers), MoRTH's RVSF/ATS licence-plus-mandated-demand tracks (rules amended Apr/May-2026), defence's DTIS and SRIJAN Positive-Indigenisation demand reservation, and MIB's film incentive of up to 40%/Rs 30cr.
details: Layer 10 (interlinkages): 23 stack/exclude/converge/feed edges -- MeitY PLI FAQ Clause 3.6 is the explicit legal basis for PLI+SPECS+state stacking; PMFME<->AIF has a formal convergence SOP; RoDTEP and RoSCTL are mutually exclusive per shipment; RoDTEP for AA/EOU/SEZ was restored 01-Jun-2025 at segregated 4RE rates. Key structural finding: NO aggregator, official or private, maintains stacking information systematically -- myscheme.gov.in is citizen-facing and API-locked; NSWS is the only real company-facing aggregator; stacking knowledge lives in guideline PDFs and subsidy-consultant sites.

Layer 11 (PRS): budget-utilization reality for 12 ministries -- Semicon India 23%->9%->~61% utilization; IT-Hardware PLI 2.0 at 3% of production target while LSEM beat its investment target; BharatNet 75% underspent with 0.8% of hotspots active; PMAY-U 19% utilization 2024-25; two 2025-26 budget schemes (High-Yielding Seeds, Mission for Pulses) died at zero utilization. PRS blind spot: no Demand-for-Grants analysis has EVER covered Commerce/DPIIT, Heavy Industries, Textiles, Steel, Chemicals, or MSME -- the manufacturing-incentive core.

Two displayed contradictions flagged for resolution before citation: coal-gasification incentive rate (15%-of-capex per coal.gov.in vs 20% in explainers) and 'NIPU' -- an acronym in our own scheme list that no public source recognizes.

Honest negatives kept as data: ELI has no page on labour.gov.in and epfindia.gov.in was unreachable by every channel; Tourism's Budget-2025 hotel infra-lending measures have no on-site operationalization artifact; Bharat Small Reactors appear nowhere on dae.gov.in; IN-SPACe's seed fund/VC fund are unverifiable behind a ServiceNow JS shell.

Reusable access discovery: much of the government web estate is JS shells over headless WordPress at cms-<ministry>.digifootprint.gov.in/wp-json (powermin, mohua, jalshakti, dsir, moes, tourism, msde, mdoner) -- with per-site exceptions documented (dfpd.gov.in needs a real browser; ayush.gov.in's schemes live in its Angular JS bundle; birac.nic.in resolves only via pinned IP 164.100.228.150).

chronological_deliverables.title: State-government incentive sweep: layer 12 of the digital twin -- 30 states/UTs, 117 site-verified policies
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: Six parallel agents swept state single-window and industries-department websites (first launch killed en masse by the usage limit; relaunched verbatim after reset). The layer's central thesis-confirming result: the central-to-state incentive stack is now PRIMARY-SOURCE-verified in four places -- UP's ECMS top-up states verbatim 'Equal to Central ECMS incentives' (doubling the central payout), Assam's Electronics Policy 2023 adds 40% on top of central ISM capex assistance (the Tata OSAT package, gazette-verified), AP's IDP 4.0 pays a PLI top-up of 10% of the central PLI award, and Kerala pays a 20% PLI-investor top-up. What layer 10 had only from consultant sites is now on government pages.
details: A 2025-26 state policy wave documented: Maharashtra MIISP 2025 (the actual PSI-2019 successor, GR 31-12-2025) plus five new Nov-Dec 2025 sector policies (GCC, AVGC-XR, MATRIX, Gems & Jewellery, Bamboo); Punjab IBDP 2026 (gazette 08-03-2026, supersedes 2022); Uttarakhand MIIP 2025; Karnataka Industrial Policy 2025-30 (shift to a capex-vs-production-linked choice: 2.5/2.0/1.0% of turnover by zone, 60%-of-VFA cap); MP's Feb-2025 policy family (IPP with the BIPA 40%->10% EFCI formula, EV, Semiconductor, Drone); Viksit Gujarat Industrial Policy 2026; Mizoram 2025; Meghalaya MIIPP 2024; Telangana Next-Gen Life Sciences 2026-30.

Fully quantified packages: TN's four mutually-exclusive options (100% SGST 15y / fixed capital by district / flexible to 40% EFA / turnover-linked); Rajasthan RIPS 2024 (irreversible choice: 75% SGST-7y, 13-28% capital, 1.2-2% turnover + boosters); Odisha IPR 2022 (UNCAPPED 20-30% capital subsidy, 20-year green-hydrogen power package); J&K NCSS + IP 2021-30 stack (30-50% capital, 100% GST-linked 10y capped 300% of P&M, 100% state SGST 10y) -- including an internal inconsistency flagged inside the official J&K PDF (working-capital subvention stated two different ways).

Honest gaps recorded as data: Telangana and Delhi have NO current umbrella industrial policy; West Bengal's reported 2025 large-industry scheme unverifiable (Silpasathi states only MSME + Tourism grant schemes exist); Bihar's policy web presence dead outside Udyami/BIADA; Manipur (site refuses connections) and Arunachal (JS shell) unverifiable; Goa still lists an interest-subsidy scheme that textually lapsed in 2011.

Two hijacked/squatted domains discovered and flagged everywhere (README, cluster files, session memory): wbidc.com (suspended, iframing an ad domain) and investinassam.com (gambling-link blog) -- real portals are silpasathi.wb.gov.in and eodb.assam.gov.in. Additional dead/wrong domains: invest.karnataka.gov.in (real: investkarnataka.co.in), morth.nic.in, udyog.bihar.gov.in, dic.nagaland.gov.in, goa-idc.com.

Flagged for follow-up: OCR the two scanned policy PDFs (Viksit Gujarat 2026, Punjab IBDP 2026 gazette); verify UNNATI 2024 registration status (Nagaland's page states the window ran to 31-Mar-2026, i.e. already lapsed); Haryana incentive texts sit behind the investharyana.in login (names recovered from the public JS bundle only).

chronological_deliverables.title: OCR extraction of the two scanned state policies -- Viksit Gujarat 2026 and Punjab IBDP 2026 fully quantified
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: The two policies flagged un-extractable in phase 40 were OCR'd end-to-end (pdftoppm 150dpi + tesseract over all 107 pages) and turned out to be ENGLISH throughout -- the language check found the only vernacular content is the Punjabi script inside Punjab's state emblem, so no translation was needed. The Gujarat file was image-only merely because it is a CorelDRAW brochure export; the Punjab gazette is scanned print. Both full incentive matrices are now machine-readable in the twin.
details: Viksit Gujarat 2026: choose-your-combination structure (capital subsidy + 7% interest subsidy + Rs 1-2/unit power tariff) inside taluka-graded eFCI ceilings -- MSME 35/45%, Large-thrust 25/35%, Mega 30/35% (Rs 1,000cr + 250 jobs + thrust), Ultra-Mega 35/40% (Rs 10,000cr + 3,000 jobs), and a special 45/50% ceiling for toys/footwear/robots/drones/sports goods. EPF 100% reimbursed 5-10y; stamp duty waived Ultra-Mega only; SC/ST +5% ceiling. 21 thrust sectors including two new signals vs Aatmanirbhar-2022: nuclear power equipment (SMRs) and semiconductor ancillaries (UHP chemicals/gases). R&D early-bird: first 5 centres >= Rs 300cr get 50% capital subsidy up to Rs 50cr/yr + Rs 10,000/person/month payroll subsidy.

Punjab IBDP 2026 (gazette 8-Mar-2026): overall ceiling 100% of FCI (125% in Border/Kandi districts or the 9 priority sectors), absolute cap Rs 500cr, one-time irrevocable 10-15y eligibility-period choice with lapse-if-unused annual ceilings. Heads: 100% stamp + electricity duty exemption, capital subsidy 20% FCI cap Rs 10cr, 75% net-SGST reimbursement, employment subsidy Rs 3,000-4,000/mo per Punjab-resident employee 5y (floor Rs 25cr FCI + 50 jobs), CGTMSE fee 100% reimbursed, and a distinctive paddy-straw-boiler subsidy (50%, up to Rs 7.5cr) that sits ABOVE the overall ceiling. Mega thresholds: FCI > Rs 500cr, IT/ITeS > Rs 250cr, or the first 3 GCCs creating 1,000+ jobs.
Both cluster entries in layer 12 upgraded from 'not extractable' to fully quantified; README OCR flags cleared; raw structured schedules in ocr_extracts_gj_pb.json with method note.

chronological_deliverables.title: Canonical data model, 312-instrument flat index, source flowchart, auto-update engine -- and UNNATI verified CLOSED-oversubscribed
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: The twin's accumulated data now has a clean extraction model: nine keyed entity types (OfferingEntity, Instrument, ApplicationWindow, InterlinkageEdge, BudgetObservation, Company, Pairing, SourceRoute, append-only Observation) that every sweep maps into, plus a flat query-ready index normalizing ALL 312 instruments (195 central + 117 state) from catalog v1, v2 and the state layer into one schema. Identity rules: renames tracked as successor_of (LSEM->MPMS, PSI-2019->MIISP-2025, TS-iPASS->TG-iPASS); central instruments never merged with their state top-ups (linked via stacks edges); 'not published' is a valid observation, not a missing row.
details: UNNATI 2024 live-verified on unnati.dpiit.gov.in: registration STOPPED because applications exceeded the funding allocated to the states (all 8 NE states) -- stronger than the lapsed 31-Mar-2026 window; disposal of registered applications by 30-09-2026; Amendment dated 01-Apr-2026 on portal; resumes only if rejections free funds. Status corrected in the NE cluster and flat index. Oversubscription is itself a demand signal for a NE scheme.

Auto-update engine (layer 14): per-source refresh design -- PIB daily (pib_index.py exists), NSWS + RBI WSS archive + UNNATI notice-diff weekly, top-20 instrument pages monthly, full agent sweep + PRS DFG + DPIIT FDI quarterly fact sheets quarterly; event triggers on Cabinet PRID keyword match, gazette uploads and UNNATI notice text change. Pipeline: fetch -> Observation rows -> diff -> corrections DISPLAYED with superseded_on -> rebuild index + bulletins.

Orbis (Bureau van Dijk) mapped as the target source for the twin's known coverage gap (unlisted companies, ownership trees, GUO): license-blocked, no free API; the Company entity carries a bvd_id slot ready for it; free partial proxies named -- GLEIF LEI, MCA master data, OpenCorporates. Current company layer remains Damodaran + yfinance + screener.in.

Information flowchart (docs/DATA_MODEL.md, mermaid, renders on GitHub): 8 source families -> 9 model entities -> 4 product families (flat index, bulletins, quarterly Trade Watch + FY series, investor map), with the refresh-cadence table and the corrections rule.

chronological_deliverables.title: Auto-update scripts live-tested + portal directory, abbreviations glossary, generated scheme catalogue, requirements
chronological_deliverables.repo: digital-twin-for-ipa (scripts/refresh_twin.py, scripts/build_catalogue.py, layers/15_directory.json, docs/, state/, requirements.txt)
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: The update engine (layer 14) is now executable: scripts/refresh_twin.py implements every cadence as a subcommand -- routes (parallel health check of all 64 directory portals), unnati (notice-text diff, the resumption watch), nsws, rbi (latest WSS issue), pib (delegates to the policy repo's indexer), catalogue (rebuild), weekly (bundle). Snapshots are append-only dated JSONs in state/ with greppable CHANGE lines on any diff -- the corrections-displayed rule enforced in code. LIVE-TESTED: 64 portals checked (4 down, all matching known access quirks), UNNATI registration_stopped=True correctly detected, RBI WSS resolved to the Jul-20-2026 issue after a date-format regex fix. First snapshots committed as evidence.
details: Portal directory (layer 15 + docs/DIRECTORY.md): 27 central + 37 state portals with per-portal access notes, plus 12 known-bad domains (hijacked wbidc.com / squatted investinassam.com / NXDOMAIN traps) flagged never-cite; the machine version feeds the routes health check.

Abbreviations glossary (docs/ABBREVIATIONS.md): ~130 entries across program/institutions, central instruments, state policies, and the data layer -- including the collision traps (AIF agri-fund vs SEBI AIF; Odisha IPR-2022 vs intellectual property; FCI food-corporation vs fixed-capital-investment).

Scheme catalogue (docs/SCHEME_CATALOGUE.md): generated view of all 312 instruments grouped by tier and entity with status flags (open/closed/closed-oversubscribed/lapsed) and one-line company benefit; regenerated by scripts/build_catalogue.py, never hand-edited.

requirements.txt: automation scripts are deliberately stdlib-only (curl via subprocess); system tools documented (poppler, tesseract); optional pypdf/pandas for analysis extras; companion-repo dependencies cross-referenced (weasyprint under homebrew python, yfinance in the investor map).
Crontab lines documented in the README (daily PIB 07:17, weekly Mon 08:23) but NOT auto-installed -- deliberate, given this machine's launchd/TCC history.

chronological_deliverables.title: Cron jobs installed -- the twin's auto-update cadences are now live on this machine
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: The two documented cadences were installed into the user crontab and verified: daily 07:17 PIB register update (refresh_twin.py pib -> policy repo's pib_index.py --update) and weekly Monday 08:23 full check (64-portal route health + UNNATI notice diff + NSWS + RBI WSS). Verified two ways: crontab -l shows both entries, and a dry-run under a cron-like minimal environment (env -i with the crontab's PATH) executed cleanly, resolving the Jul-20-2026 RBI WSS issue.
details: Both jobs log to state/cron.log; snapshots continue landing as append-only dated JSONs in state/ with CHANGE lines on diffs.

Why this scheduler should work where the old launchd job never fired: these are cron (not launchd) jobs, and both repos live in the home root -- NOT in TCC-protected ~/Downloads, which was what killed the daily_combined_report launchd job with permission denials.

Known limitation accepted: cron silently skips runs while the Mac is asleep/off at the scheduled minute (no launchd-style catch-up); daytime slots (07:17 / Mon 08:23) chosen to maximize hit rate. Health check after a quiet week: state/cron.log + whether new dated snapshots appear in state/.
pib_index.py confirmed stdlib-only (urllib/sqlite3), so /usr/bin/python3 runs both jobs without any package installs.

chronological_deliverables.title: n8n workflow for the twin's auto-refresh -- CHANGE detection, email alerting, snapshot commits
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: An importable n8n workflow now wraps the same refresh_twin.py engine the crontab runs, adding what plain cron cannot: a Code node that parses run output for CHANGE/DOWN lines and raises a synthetic same-day alert if the UNNATI portal notice ever flips to registration_stopped=False (the resumption signal); an email-alert node targeting the user's Gmail (SMTP credential to be attached in the n8n UI -- placeholder only, no real creds in the repo per the secrets rule); automatic LOCAL commits of state/ snapshots after each weekly run (commit only, never push -- pushing stays a human action); and a manual trigger for on-demand runs through the full alert pipeline. Timezone pinned Asia/Kolkata.
details: Wiring: Daily-07:17 -> pib; Weekly-Mon-08:23 + Manual -> weekly -> Detect-CHANGE -> IF -> (Email -> Commit) / Commit.

Scheduler-overlap decision documented in workflows/README.md: keep cron primary (zero-dependency, fires without n8n alive) and use n8n's manual trigger for alerting runs, OR activate n8n schedules and remove the two crontab lines -- n8n only fires while its process runs, so it would need a keep-alive.

n8n itself: npm install -g n8n kicked off (large package); workflow import (n8n import:workflow) pending install completion -- the JSON is the durable artifact either way and imports into any n8n instance.

chronological_deliverables.title: n8n local install -- Node-26 incompatibility found and worked around via nvm Node 22
chronological_deliverables.repo: machine setup (no repo changes; workflow JSON already pushed in phase 45)
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: The first npm install -g n8n under Homebrew Node v26 FAILED SILENTLY: exit code 0 but npm rolled the package back because n8n's isolated-vm native dependency does not compile on Node 26 (n8n targets LTS 20/22) -- leaving only a dangling /opt/homebrew/bin/n8n symlink (removed). This 'exit-0 but rolled back' failure mode is worth remembering for any global npm install with native deps.
details: Workaround in progress at time of writing: Node 22.23.1 installed via the machine's existing nvm; npm install -g n8n re-running under that toolchain (background).

Next step once it lands: n8n import:workflow --input=workflows/n8n_twin_refresh.json, then verify registration; SMTP credential attachment and Active toggle remain manual UI steps by design (no real creds in repo).
Scheduler stance unchanged: crontab stays primary; n8n adds the CHANGE-detection/email/commit pipeline and on-demand manual runs.

chronological_deliverables.title: n8n installed under Node 22 and the twin workflow imported -- TwinRefreshIPA001 registered
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: n8n installed cleanly under nvm Node 22.23.1 (~/.nvm/versions/node/v22.23.1/bin/n8n) after the Node-26 isolated-vm build failure, and the workflow imported and verified: n8n list:workflow shows 'TwinRefreshIPA001 | Digital Twin IPA -- auto-refresh'. The import surfaced one final format requirement -- a top-level workflow id field (SQLITE NOT-NULL constraint without it) -- now fixed in the repo JSON and pushed.
details: Imported INACTIVE by design: the crontab remains the primary scheduler; activating n8n's schedules would double-fire the jobs. Usage: PATH=~/.nvm/versions/node/v22.23.1/bin:$PATH n8n -> http://localhost:5678 -> attach SMTP credential to the email node -> use Manual-run trigger for on-demand refreshes with CHANGE-detection + alerting, or toggle Active and remove the two crontab lines.
Timezone pinned Asia/Kolkata in workflow settings; the dangling /opt/homebrew/bin/n8n symlink from the failed Node-26 attempt was removed.

chronological_deliverables.title: Email alerting live and tested -- credential-free design via runtime plist sourcing
chronological_deliverables.repo: digital-twin-for-ipa (scripts/send_twin_alert.py; cron weekly pipe; n8n workflow re-imported)
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: Rather than attaching the Gmail app password to n8n's credential store (which would mean handling the secret in plain text -- the established credential boundary), the alert path was built credential-free: send_twin_alert.py sources GMAIL_USER/GMAIL_APP_PASSWORD/MAIL_TO at runtime from the environment, falling back to the EnvironmentVariables block of the machine's existing dailybrief launchd plist. The secret flows plist -> process -> Gmail and never entered the session context (redacted at the shell), the repo, n8n's store, or any log.
details: Tested with three delivered emails: basic --test send; silent path verified (no CHANGE lines -> no email); real alert format triggered by a synthetic CHANGE line ('[digital-twin] 1 change(s) detected'). Alert body includes CHANGE lines, portals-down list, and raw tail; the UNNATI registration_stopped=False flip is wired as a synthetic high-priority CHANGE.

Cron: the weekly Monday line now pipes refresh output through send_twin_alert.py --if-changes (tee to cron.log preserved). Honest process note: the crontab rewrite took four attempts -- the daily line's 'cd' prefix kept getting dropped in regenerated content; a Python-side assertion (count of cd-prefix == 2) caught it and the final install is verified correct.

n8n: the emailSend node was replaced with a credential-free executeCommand calling the same script; TwinRefreshIPA001 re-imported successfully -- the workflow now needs NO credential setup at all. workflows/README.md updated to document the design.

chronological_deliverables.title: End-to-end weekly pipeline test -- validated by a real, organic route-recovery event
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: The full weekly pipeline was run once under the exact installed cron invocation (env -i with the crontab's PATH) and validated itself with a REAL event rather than a synthetic trigger: UP Nivesh Mitra had come back online since the afternoon snapshot, producing 'CHANGE route UP Nivesh Mitra: ok=False -> True (200)', which flowed through CHANGE detection and delivered a genuine alert email ('[digital-twin] 1 change(s) detected 2026-07-20') to the user's inbox. Exit 0 end to end.
details: Full chain in one pass: 64 routes checked (3 down -- DSIR CMS, BIRAC, Silpasathi, all known access quirks) -> UNNATI still registration_stopped=True -> NSWS pages live -> RBI WSS current at Jul-20-2026 -> tee to state/cron.log -> --if-changes alert -> email delivered.
Snapshot-diff mechanics proven: same-day re-run compared against the afternoon snapshot and caught the state flip -- exactly the behavior the Monday runs will exhibit week-over-week.
Evidence committed and pushed. Next scheduled firings: daily PIB 07:17 tomorrow; weekly Monday 27-Jul 08:23.

chronological_deliverables.title: Interlinkage graph verified against Drishti IAS + 6 other IAS sources -- 12 confirmed, 3 resolved, 1 disputed, NIPU-2026 vindicated
chronological_deliverables.repo: digital-twin-for-ipa (layers/10_interlinkages.json: verification block, 12 new edges, 3 corrections)
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: Two agents checked all 23 edges' RELATIONSHIP claims against drishtiias.com and vajiram/pwonlyias/insights/clearias/forumias/nextias/visionias. Twelve edges confirmed (AIF-PMFME 'over and above the 35% subsidy' verbatim on two source families; ECMS-ISM boundary; RoDTEP halt-and-restoration; RDI two-tier design; SIGHT fertilizer offtake; UCF leverage; KUSUM-SuryaGhar; Gati Shakti fundless meta-layer; UP state top-up stacking). Best result: NIPU-2026 -- Drishti concluded our register entry was a mis-entry (truncated 'NIPUN'), but the PIB register proves the acronym is OFFICIAL, in the Cabinet release title itself (PRID 2284800, 15-Jul-2026, National Investment Policy for Urea-2026). The aggregators are five days behind the primary source -- recorded as the standing demonstration of why register entries never get dropped on aggregator silence.
details: Resolved: (1) ECMS-MPMS boundary -- finished phones (sales-linked 2.25-5%) vs component layer, coupled by MPMS's +1.5% domestic-sourcing bonus, reclassified as a feeds edge; (2) shipbuilding arithmetic -- two independent sources corroborate IIF Rs 5,000cr sitting INSIDE MDF Rs 25,000cr (MIF 20,000 equity + IIF 5,000), so 24,736+25,000+19,989 = 69,725 exactly and our 74,725 reading is superseded (displayed correction).

Downgraded to DISPUTED: coal-gasification incentive percentage -- the secondary sources contradict EACH OTHER (Drishti: 20% of P&M in 4 instalments for the Rs 8,500cr scheme; PWOnlyIAS: 15% of capex or lump sum whichever lower in 2 instalments from the same Feb-2024 guidelines; VisionIAS-family ties 20%/4 to the separate Rs 37,500cr surface-gasification scheme). No percentage will be cited until read from the coal.gov.in guideline PDF.

Still open: SEZ+PLI eligibility (no source resolves it; new context: SEZ Rule 18 amended Apr-2026 allowing DTA sales for semiconductor/component SEZ units). Silent-but-unopposed: same-capex PLI+SPECS stacking (sole anchor remains the OFFICIAL MeitY FAQ Clause 3.6) and EPCG+PLI.

12 new edges added (all SECONDARY): strongest is PM E-DRIVE's stackability with PLI-Auto/PLI-ACC written INTO the scheme terms ('independent of and in addition to'); plus MPMS->ECMS demand-pull, ECMS as horizontal enabler across the 6-scheme electronics stack, AIF's GENERAL convergence clause (any Central/State scheme), AIF+CGTMSE and AHIDF+CGS guarantee layers, EBP GST-cut tax stack, RDI's nested FoF third layer, UP-Semiconductor||ISM per-layer state top-ups, MEIS->RoDTEP lineage, proposed gasification GST-cess reimbursement, and one flagged-for-verification possible ELI->PM-VBRY rename.

Status corrections: ISM 2.0's Rs 1,000cr (PRS layer) clarified as the FY27 operational allocation only -- approved Semicon 2.0 outlay is Rs 1.27 lakh cr (Cabinet 15-Jul-2026), consistent with catalog v1. Spot-checks confirmed: E-DRIVE e-2W to 31-Jul-2026, MPMS Rs 62,500cr, ECMS 22,919->40,000 sequence. RDI first disbursals: no source reports money reaching fund managers yet (nothing later than Nov-2025).

chronological_deliverables.title: EV decks gain state-arrears + leads slides; machine Python resolution fixed to 3.14

chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: (1) Both EV decks extended with one design-matched slide each, built from twin layers: the Market Evaluation deck (now 11 slides) gains 'State Incentive Reality Check' -- the arrears divide (Telangana Rs 3,736cr confirmed arrears, AP Rs 3,000-5,000cr FIFO-promised) vs the payers (Rajasthan record Rs 765.78cr FY25, MP Rs 695cr on-stage) vs the opaque (MH/KA publish no disbursal series), closing on the EV-siting implication; the Policy Gaps deck (now 9 slides) gains '07 -- The Counterparties' -- report-verified HIGH leads (Suzuki's own-report Yen 1.2T India capex/42% of revenue, Hyundai Mobis 6x order target + Bengaluru SW branch, Delta's ~$500M Krishnagiri campus) plus 'Batteries: the open seat' (LGES-JSW non-binding, Samsung SDI scouting, CATL blocked both ways by PN3 + Beijing export controls). chronological_deliverables.title: Annual-report sweep extended to 4 uncovered market clusters; ministry-wise reportage view added
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: (1) The India/APAC intent analysis reached the markets the earlier rounds never touched -- ASEAN (Indonesia/Thailand/Malaysia/Philippines/Vietnam), Gulf-MENA ex-Saudi, Europe's thin tail (IT/ES/PT/BE/IE/PL/GR/NO/AT), LatAm ex-Brazil, and Turkey/New Zealand -- 60 yfinance-profitable large caps whose OWN latest annual report was mined for India/APAC investment language. 58 merged into the leads layer (2 deduped) -> 321 leads across 45 countries; targeting shortlist now 79 priority + 57 warm. Thirteen new HIGH names, each on its own filing: Delta Electronics Thailand (board-approved INR 1,212m India investment Jul-2025, Bengaluru HQ+R&D, Krishnagiri SEZ tax-exempt to 2034), Danieli (Sri City plant, Rs 2,511cr India order book), Fincantieri (CEO: India 'a market of central importance in our long-term international growth strategy', EUR 200m+ Indian Navy torpedo contract), Indra (EUR 55m+ AAI air-traffic systems, Bengaluru radar factory with Tata Advanced Systems), Kongsberg (Kochi waterjet plant, 18 waterjets for Indian Navy NGMVs), Andritz (seven Indian pumped-storage projects via local factories), SCG ('SCG's first manufacturing operation in India' -- AAC plant JV, Gujarat), SQM (20-F discloses SQM India Pvt Ltd incorporated 22-Apr-2024, Pune), ADNOC Gas (three long-term India LNG SPAs -- IOCL 14-yr, GAIL and HPCL 10-yr each), Kerry Group ('looking at our next phase of investment in India'), Grupo Bimbo (one of six new 2024 plants built in India, 14 Indian bakeries), Iveco (INBOUND special situation -- Tata Motors' ~EUR 3.8bn tender offer makes India ~35% of combined revenue), and Arcelik, whose 2024 report says VoltasBeko 'has set its sights on becoming a leading brand in India's home appliance market' through the 49% VoltBek JV with Voltas (Sanand, Gujarat plant + R&D office, 2.8M+ refrigerators since 2020, now exporting to Bangladesh and Sri Lanka).
details: Grade distribution of the 58 merged leads: 13 HIGH, 20 MEDIUM, 15 LOW, 10 NONE. Cluster-level yields: Europe's tail was richest (7 HIGH of 14 -- engineering and defence), ASEAN weakest relative to its size (2 HIGH of 14; Indonesia produced none at all), Gulf/MENA 1 of 12, LatAm 2 of 10, Turkey/NZ 1 of 10.

SECOND-ORDER FINDING (bigger than the leads framing shows): Gulf petrochemicals are quietly India-dependent by revenue share rather than by investment. Industries Qatar's fertilizer (QAFCO) segment saw the Indian sub-continent jump from 10% to 22% of segment revenue in ONE year (segment NP +36%), with QatarEnergy committing to double urea capacity to 12.4 MTPA; Mesaieed Petrochemical runs 26% of group revenue and 54% of its chlor-alkali segment into the sub-continent. Honest caveat recorded: no named Indian counterparty appears -- the urea relationship is contracted at QatarEnergy Marketing level (Muntajat consolidated into it in 2024), so IQ's own disclosures show the exposure only in geography charts.

NEGATIVE signals captured, not just positive: Thai Union is REDUCING its Avanti Feeds stake (24.21% -> 19.81%, Mar-2025), UCB's APAC direction is divestment ($680m China business sold to CBC/Mubadala, Nov-2024), Umicore's Asia battery-materials assets took 2024 impairments with Chinese CAM assumed to stay underutilized, and Orbia -- the strongest India story in LatAm (Netafim plants, Dura-Line, completed Vectus buyout) -- signed a Mar-2025 MoU to SELL its India pipes business to Supreme Industries. Hoa Phat carries both directions at once: its HRC won exemption from India's DGTR anti-dumping duty ('a strong competitive advantage in the Indian market') while the same report names cheap Indian steel as a threat.

HONESTY RULES HELD THROUGHOUT. NONE is recorded as a finding, never imputed: Astra International and Barito Renewables are explicitly Indonesia-focused in their own words, Orlen's Strategy 2035 is entirely CEE/Atlantic, Grupo Mexico has zero India mentions and Americas-only capex. Unprofitable names were EXCLUDED rather than promoted -- Orbia (TTM margin -5.7%, flagged top re-test candidate), Ezz Steel (FY23 net loss), Indorama Ventures, Petronas Chemicals, PTT Global Chemical, Energy Absolute, VinFast, Stellantis, Talgo, Solvay/Syensqo -- each with the reason stated in its cluster file's excluded block. Three circulating rumours were checked and DISCARDED rather than repeated: the 'Indomie India variant', an LG tie-up at ANTAM, and Delta's widely-quoted ~USD 500m India figure (press-only, absent from every filing -- labelled as such).

SOURCE-ACCESS REALITY LOGGED PER COMPANY in report_used: annual-report PDFs were bot-blocked or oversized for Danieli, Fincantieri, Indra, EDP, ICBP, ANTAM, Chandra Asri, Top Glove, Umicore and UCB -- those quotes come from official press releases, rating-agency documents (CARE Ratings for Danieli India) or Wayback-archived copies of the identical official PDFs (SCG, Thai Union), each flagged. Infrastructure finding: yfinance does NOT cover ADX/DFM at all (.AE tickers 404), so every UAE name carries UNVERIFIED profitability with annual-report figures; PSE returns null (URC's profitability was verified from its own SEC Form 17-C: Php12.5bn net income, ~7.7% margin); Turkish TAS-29 inflation accounting distorts ratios where it applies.

TURKEY/NZ SPECIFICS: Arcelik is the cluster's only HIGH but is marked UNVERIFIED on the profitability screen -- TAS 29 hyperinflation accounting turns a TRY 1.69bn parent-attributable profit into a negative yfinance margin/ROE via a TRY 28.3bn non-cash monetary loss; the same caveat is recorded per Turkish name (Erdemir's largely USD-functional accounts are less distorted; Tofas's +188% 'revenue growth' is a nominal-TRY artifact). Sisecam MEDIUM on its 100%-owned Halol, Gujarat flat-glass plant ('updated its strategies in India, a key global market'); Fisher & Paykel Healthcare MEDIUM (Bengaluru subsidiary, APAC revenue NZ$420.8m +14%); a2 Milk MEDIUM on SE Asia with ZERO India mentions across 156 pages; Aselsan MEDIUM with zero India, consistent with strained Turkey-India relations. Fonterra LOW -- it exited the Future Dairy India JV in 2022 and is divesting its Asian consumer arm to Lactalis. A rumour was caught and killed here too: the supposed Ford Otosan-Sanand engine deal is a conflation (Sanand went to Tata; Ford Otosan bought Craiova, Romania) -- Ford Otosan, Tofas and Ulker are honest NONEs.

TOOLING: scripts/merge_cluster_leads.py folds staged cluster files into the leads layer using the SAME score as the original build (profitability 40 + expansion 25 + open lane 25 + state top-up 10), deduping on ticker then name so re-runs are idempotent -- which let the late-arriving Turkey/NZ cluster fold in cleanly afterwards (10 more leads, 0 duplicates). Raw per-cluster evidence archived under layers/16_enrichment/clusters/. Commit ee85a06.

PROCESS NOTE worth keeping: five coordinator agents each fanned out to sub-researchers. Four delivered promptly; the Turkey/NZ coordinator went 36 minutes with a 129-byte transcript and was judged dead -- queued messages to a stopped agent are never delivered, so detection means stat-ing the transcript for growth rather than trusting the absence of an error. A replacement was launched, and then the ORIGINAL completed after ~41 minutes and delivered a full 10-company file. The duplicate was stopped before it could overwrite the same path. Lesson recorded: a silent agent is not necessarily a dead one, and a replacement writing to the same output path is a race -- give the replacement a distinct path or stop the original first.

ALSO THIS PHASE (separate request): the quarterly reportage page gained a ministry-wise companion view. docs/reportage_ministry.html groups all 3,982 mapped announcements under their scheme's OWNING ministry (49 of them, Cabinet/CCEA rows filed under the line ministry, register attribution kept inline per row) with each row time-stamped by release date, newest first. The original quarter-wise docs/reportage.html was RESTORED unchanged from 096720b after the first attempt overwrote it -- the user asked for a separate file, not a replacement -- and the two now cross-link. Each has its own template; build_reportage.py emits both every run, so the weekly cron keeps them in sync. Commits 1e4d30b, 0da6312.

chronological_deliverables.title: PARIVESH beyond EC: Forest / Wildlife / CRZ registers pulled and analysed

Forest Clearance (28,999 proposals) carries forest_area in hectares -- the land-QUANTUM measure EC lacks: 49,641 ha (122,666 acres) of forest with final diversion orders granted, a SECOND land-creation channel beside the ~253,720-ac industrial bank. chronological_deliverables.title: Land-price correlation: does implementation bid up land? Sign REVERSES
chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: 8-state cross-section vs NHB RESIDEX: every industrial-activity metric correlates NEGATIVELY with residential price growth; forest-land granted = -0.78 Pearson, -0.85 controlling GSDP. Not proof industry fails to lift land value -- the measurable price signal lives in a different sub-market (services metros), industrial land is administered-priced, and heavy industry sites where land is cheap (reverse causation). Proper test needs industrial circle-rate/allotment-rate panels, stated on the page.

chronological_deliverables.title: IBEF matching: the quasi-official channel promotes the themes, misses the executors

chronological_deliverables.headline_finding: All 41 matrix companies checked against ibef.org (Commerce-Ministry trust).
